name: TuolumneCG
desc: Tuolumne Meadows Campground, walk-in sites, water, 3/10 mile SW of PCT.

name: TuolumneStore
desc: Tuolumne Meadows has a nearby campground with backpacker sites, store [3/10 mile W of the PCT, surprisingly well stocked], snack bar, outfitter [in the gas station], and a post office [209-372-8236]. The store and post office may not be open early season due to snow.

name: Tuolumne_Meadows
desc: Tuolumne Meadows camp store and post office, 3/10 mile W. Start of NorCal section.

name: TR0948B
desc: Trail junction to Glen Aulin campground over a wooden bridge. Water, bear lockers, outhouse nearby.

name: WACS0948
desc: Glen Aulin backpacker campground. Water from nearby creek, bear lockers, outhouse nearby.

name: WACS0965
desc: Last ford of Wilson Creek, small tent site nearby.

name: TR0973
desc: Benson Lake Trail, lake and campsites 4/10 mile SW.

name: WACS0987
desc: Wilma Lake [sometimes incorrectly named Wilmer Lake], campsites. This lake has a history of name changes on maps and trail signs. The lake is named after Wilma Seavey, daughter of Clyde L. Seavey.

name: WACS1006
desc:
Stream, campsites nearby. Last creek northbound in Kennedy Canyon.
---
WATER ALERT: Next northbound water after the Last creek northbound in Kennedy Canyon may be Sardine Creek in 10.4 miles.

name: WACS1060
desc: Tributary of Pleasant Valley Creek, small campsite nearby.

name: WACS1063
desc: Lilly Pad Lake, a few campsites nearby.

name: Echo_Lake_Chalet
desc: Echo Lake Chalet, small store on PCT. Water taxi across Echo Lake. Accepts packages.

name: WACS1102
desc: Susie Lake outlet, campsites. Campsites also south and north of the outlet.

name: TR1108
desc: Dicks Lake trail junction, camping nearby.

name: CS1122
desc: Seasonal stream below Bear Lake, campsite nearby.

name: TR1130
desc: Tahoe Rim trail departs the PCT, campsites nearby.

name: PeterGrubb
desc: Peter Grubb Hut, shelter, outhouse, campsite.

name: WACS1167
desc: White Rock Creek, wooden bridge, several campsites.

name: WACS1172
desc: Seasonal Lacey Creek, campsites nearby, sign says "Always water 3/10 mile downstream."

name: WACS1191
desc: Milton Creek, wooden bridge, campsite nearby.

name: WACS1192
desc: 2nd crossing of Milton Creek, wooden bridge, small campsite nearby.

name: WA1193
desc: Haypress Creek, steel bridge. Trail junction to Wild Plum Campground and side trail into Sierra City nearby.

name: Sierra_City
desc: Sierra City, ~0.5mi from Hwy 49 junction. General store, hotel. Common resupply.

name: WBranchNelsonCreek
desc: Seasonal W Branch Nelson Creek, campsite.

name: EHopkinsSeep
desc: Seasonal East Hopkins Seep, small campsite nearby.

name: AlderSpring
desc: Alder Spring (800 feet off trail) trail junction, campsite nearby.

name: MForkFeatherRiver
desc: Middle Fork Feather River, steel bridge, campsites on N bank.

name: BearCreekB
desc: Bear Creek, footbridge, large campsite, water.

name: SilverLakeTR2
desc: Silver Lake trail junction (the lake is 2 miles E), large campsite nearby.

name: Belden
desc: Belden Town Resort, on trail. Bar, small store, cabins. Accepts packages.

name: WilliamsCabinSite
desc: William's Cabin site, campsite, small creek nearby.

name: MyrtleFlat
desc: Campsites at Myrtle Flat, small stream nearby.

name: CarterCreek
desc: Seasonal Carter Creek, 1/2 mile N of PCT, campsites nearby.

name: CarterMeadowTR
desc: Carter Meadow trail junction, small campsites nearby.

name: NForkFeatherRiver
desc: North Fork Feather River, footbridge, campsites.

name: LassenNP
desc:
Lassen Volcanic National Park. Overnight backcountry users in Lassen Volcanic National Park must use a BEAR CANISTER to store food from April 15 - Nov 30. PCT hikers can hike the 19.2 miles of PCT within Lassen National Park without a bear canister if they camp outside the National Park or at Warner Valley Campground near Drakesbad Guest Ranch.
---
Overnight camping with stock is not allowed in Lassen Volcanic National Park except at developed sites with corrals.

name: Drakesbad
desc: Drakesbad Guest Ranch, hiker-friendly resort. Hot spring pool. Accepts packages.

name: WarnerValleyCG
desc: Warner Valley Campground, trash cans, outhouse, creek nearby.

name: SummitCreek
desc: Summit Lake trail junction, trailside Summit Creek nearby, several campsites.

name: LassenNP2
desc:
Lassen Volcanic National Park. Overnight backcountry users in Lassen Volcanic National Park must use a BEAR CANISTER to store food from April 15 - Nov 30. PCT hikers can hike the 19.2 miles of PCT within Lassen National Park without a bear canister if they camp outside the National Park or at Warner Valley Campground near Drakesbad Guest Ranch.
---
Overnight camping with stock is not allowed in Lassen Volcanic National Park except at developed sites with corrals.

name: HatCreekResort
desc: Hat Creek Resort, deli and post office, short walk from road crossing.

name: Old_Station
desc: Old Station, 3/10 mile W. Mini-mart, post office. JJ's Cafe nearby.

name: PL1410
desc: High voltage power line, trail nearby to Burney Mountain Guest Ranch [3/10 mile SW of PCT, burneymountainguestranch.com, 530-335-2544] is a Country Inn that offers camping, lodging, hot meals, showers, laundry, shade, swimming, wifi, water and resupply [including fuel canisters] and they accept resupply packages [$].

name: BurneyGroupCamp
desc: Burney Falls State Park Headwaters Group Campground, reservation from State Park required, $35 fee, outhouse, picnic tables, trash cans. [Camping in the $5/night hiker/biker campsites in the main campground 7/10 mile N is a better hiker option].

name: BurneyFallsSP
desc:
Burney Falls State Park is a park along the PCT with a campground, coin showers, pay phone and a small store that accepts hiker resupply packages.
---
WATER ALERT: Next southbound water after Burney Falls State Park may be the lake in 11.3 miles.

name: RockCreek
desc: Cross Rock Creek on a wood bridge. Good water, but only a few small campsites nearby.

name: CS1435
desc: Exposed campsite with a view of Mount Shasta.

name: Road38N10-2
desc: Small campsite in the saddle near unpaved Road 38N10, second crossing.

name: AshCamp
desc: Ash Camp Campground, outhouse, water from nearby creek, unpaved Road 38N11 nearby.

name: CS1477
desc: Small campsites in the abandoned roadbed.

name: Castella
desc: Castella, short walk via frontage road. Small market, post office.

name: CS1519
desc: Several small campsites with views of Castle Crags and Mount Shasta.

name: CS1520
desc: Campsite at the top of a ridge.

name: ChilcootCreek
desc: Seasonal headwaters of Chilcoot Creek, campsites.

name: MastersonSpring
desc: Small seasonal spring above Masterson Meadow, campsite nearby.

name: CS1581
desc: Campsite near an unpaved jeep road.

name: StatueCreek
desc: Seasonal creek below Statue Creek, several campsites.

name: PaynesLake
desc: Campsites at Paynes Lake, 100 yards W of PCT.

name: Etna
desc: Etna, 10.4 miles E of trail via hitch from Etna Summit. Full hiker services.

name: MarbleValleyCabin
desc: Creek near Marble Valley Cabin [locked], campsites, water, Canyon Creek Trail junction.

name: BuckhornSpring
desc: Buckhorn Spring, small unmarked spring 150 feet W of the PCT in a meadow NW of the large three-forked tree, campsite nearby.

name: CS1639
desc: Campsite at the end of a Road 45N78. Watch for poison oak as you drop to Grider Creek and Seiad Valley.

name: CS1642
desc: Campsite at the end of a road.

name: GriderCreek4
desc: Grider Creek, 4th crossing near campground, steel footbridge, swimming just N of bridge. Walk through the campground to start of 6.4 mile roadwalk to Seiad Valley.

name: Seiad_Valley
desc: Seiad Valley, on trail. Small store, cafe, RV park. Last CA resupply. Famous pancake challenge.

name: SeiadValley
desc: Seiad Valley is a very small community on the PCT with a small store, cafe, pay phone, Mid River RV Park [530-496-3400] w/ hiker camping, laundry, showers and a post office [530-496-3211].

name: CS1679
desc: Several small campsites in a saddle.

name: SheepCampSpring
desc: Sheep Camp Spring, campsites, and unpaved road nearby.

name: Callahans_Lodge
desc: Callahan's Mountain Lodge, 0.3mi off trail at I-5. Accepts resupply packages.

name: Callahans
desc: Callahan's Lodge [callahanslodge.com, 800-286-0507, 541-482-1299] is hiker friendly lodge 8/10 mile N of the PCT with a restaurant, lodging, laundry, showers, hiker camping.
